tasks.issueTitle: Add Romanian (ro) locale
tasks.issueBody:
I'd like to contribute a Romanian locale for zod v4's localization system.

  Romanian is spoken by approximately 24 million people worldwide. I've already implemented a working Romanian locale in my project covering:

  - Type errors with expected/received types
  - Size constraints (too_big/too_small) with proper grammar for strings, arrays, sets, maps, files
  - String format validations (email, URL, UUID, date, IP addresses, etc.)
  - Value errors, unrecognized keys, and other validations

  I'm happy to file a PR. Let me know if there are any guidelines I should follow for locale contributions outside the ones mentioned in the official [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).

tasks.issueTitle: .merge() silently drops .refine() — should it throw like .pick()/.omit() do?
cidrv6 JSON schema emits an incomplete pattern that rejects valid IPv6 CIDRs
Circular imports between `schemas` and `iso`
tasks.issueBody:
Issue is a little related to https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5095, https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/227

When I look into `schemas`, it imports from `iso`, which itself imports from `schemas` again. According to #227 this shouldn't be the case anymore, but I still see this on the latest version of zod.

https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/62bf4e439e287e55c843245b49f8d34b1ad024ee/packages/zod/src/v4/classic/schemas.ts#L5

https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/62bf4e439e287e55c843245b49f8d34b1ad024ee/packages/zod/src/v4/classic/iso.ts#L2

This caused issues in our build stack, although I'm not sure what the ultimate culprit is (likely outdated rollup dependency?), but in general it seems like a good idea to avoid circular dependencies.

If you're happy with that, I can have a look to resolve these circular dependencies, maybe add a test or some configuration to avoid circular dependencies in the future. What do you think?

tasks.issueTitle: Workaround for "Dynamic catch values are not supported in JSON Schema"
tasks.issueBody:
When using `z.toJSONSchema`, I noticed that `.catch((ctx) => somethingCustom(ctx))` was causing a crash. There was not much info on this edge case in docs and discussions, so I decided to create this issue just in case.

Relevant source:

https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/62bf4e439e287e55c843245b49f8d34b1ad024ee/packages/zod/src/v4/core/to-json-schema.ts#L561-L573

Relevant test:

https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/62bf4e439e287e55c843245b49f8d34b1ad024ee/packages/zod/src/v4/classic/tests/to-json-schema.test.ts#L281-L284

`z.toJSONSchema` supports `unrepresentable: "any"` which could potentially used for dynamic catch, similarly to how it's done for `transform()` and others:

https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/62bf4e439e287e55c843245b49f8d34b1ad024ee/packages/zod/src/v4/core/to-json-schema.ts#L521-L526

https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/62bf4e439e287e55c843245b49f8d34b1ad024ee/packages/zod/src/v4/core/to-json-schema.ts#L617-L628

It'd be also great if https://zod.dev/json-schema was updated to reflect the caveat of dynamic catch.

Happy to help with a PR if the above makes sense.

---

In the meantime, I [patched](https://pnpm.io/cli/patch) the logic locally like this:

```diff
} catch {
-   throw new Error("Dynamic catch values are not supported in JSON Schema");
+   // Assume that catch does not change the type of the schema
+   // Context: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5273
+   break;
}
```

It's more like `unrepresentable: "passthrough"` (which does not exist yet) rather than `unrepresentable: "any"`.

tasks.issueTitle: discriminatedUnion no longer warns with invalid variant at compile time (zod 4)
tasks.issueBody:
Tested with `zod 4.3.6`:

```ts
z.discriminatedUnion("type", [z.object({})]);
```

v4: no compiler error and fails at runtime with:

```
Invalid discriminated union option at index "0"
```

v3: compiler error

```
Type 'ZodObject<{}, "strip", ZodTypeAny, {}, {}>' is not assignable to type 'ZodDiscriminatedUnionOption<"type">'.
  Type '{}' is not assignable to type '{ type: ZodTypeAny; } & ZodRawShape'.
    Property 'type' is missing in type '{}' but required in type '{ type: ZodTypeAny; }'.
```

I would've expected a compile error here. These kind of issues are usually best to catch at compile-time.

tasks.issueTitle: Upgrading from 4.3.6 to 4.4.x causes issues with preprocess + catch
tasks.issueBody:
I am using zod to parse payload we received from URLSearchParams, which requires transforming comma-separated string values into an array of string (which can potentially be empty/undefined). In v4.3.6 and below, the following schema works:

```ts
z.object({
  area: z.preprocess(v => v ? v.toString().split(',') : [], z.array(z.string())).catch([])
})
```

And the payload is as follow:

```ts
{}
```

[v4.3.6 on Zod Playground](https://zod-playground.vercel.app?appdata=N4IgzgxgFgpgtgQxALhALwHQHsBGArGCAFwApgAdAOwAJqEAnGBZazAB0Y6whjDBIBu1ALwA%2BakID8EjESwBlIvQCWlAOYkAlBjBsANstIByADRHN1FgG0AuidYYG9BAE8SmMEtUbNvjBAQiaBJbTSoAX00QExABBD0AV14UKxBgcJA7WJh6MGUsShQQABYMAGYMADZokGUwAC0sABMAWVVlFAAzeLAYcKA), which works without any issue ✅ 

However, after upgrading to 4.4.x (4.4.0, 4.4.1, and 4.4.2), I get the following error:

```text
invalid_type - Invalid input: expected nonoptional, received undefined
```

All these versions are showing the same error:

- [v4.4.0 on Zod Playground](https://zod-playground.vercel.app?appdata=N4IgzgxgFgpgtgQxALhALwHQHsBGArGCAFwApgAdAOwAJqEAnGBZazAB0Y6whjDBIBu1ALwA%2BakID8EjESwBlIvQCWlAOYkAlBjBsANstIByADRHN1FgG0AuidYYG9BAE8SmMEtUbNvjBAQiaBJbTSoAX00QExABBD0AV14UKxBgcJA7WJh6MGUsShQQABYMUoAGaJBlMAAtLAATAFlVZRQAM3iwGHCgA)
- [v4.4.1 on Zod Playground](https://zod-playground.vercel.app?appdata=N4IgzgxgFgpgtgQxALhALwHQHsBGArGCAFwApgAdAOwAJqEAnGBZazAB0Y6whjDBIBu1ALwA%2BakID8EjESwBlIvQCWlAOYkAlBjBsANstIByADRHN1FgG0AuidYYG9BAE8SmMEtUbNvjBAQiaBJbTSoAX00QExABBD0AV14UKxBgcJA7WJh6MGUsShQQABYMUoBGaJBlMAAtLAATAFlVZRQAM3iwGHCgA)
- [v4.4.2 on Zod Playground](https://zod-playground.vercel.app?appdata=N4IgzgxgFgpgtgQxALhALwHQHsBGArGCAFwApgAdAOwAJqEAnGBZazAB0Y6whjDBIBu1ALwA%2BakID8EjESwBlIvQCWlAOYkAlBjBsANstIByADRHN1FgG0AuidYYG9BAE8SmMEtUbNvjBAQiaBJbTSoAX00QExABBD0AV14UKxBgcJA7WJh6MGUsShQQABYMUoAmaJBlMAAtLAATAFlVZRQAM3iwGHCgA)

tasks.issueTitle: The results vary depending on the position of the “optional” in the schema with preprocess since v4.4.0
`.default()` shallow-copies
Mutation of ctx param is unintuitive
tasks.issueBody:
Lines such as this in the zod codebase result in mutation of the passed context object.
```const ctx = _ctx ? Object.assign(_ctx, { direction: "backward" as const }) : { direction: "backward" as const };```

This causes weird issues if the same context object is used in multiple calls.

E.g.

```
const myOptions = { reportInput: true } as const;
z.decode(stringToDateCodec, someDateString, myOptions);
z.decode(stringToDateCodec, someOtherDateString, myOptions);
```

In my particular case it was causing a later decode call to run in reverse which passed a string to the encode function instead of a date.

The reason I am sharing an options object is because i'm trying to mimic global reportInput: true by simply passing it explicity in each call.

tasks.issueTitle: "სტრინგი" is not correct translation for word "string" in ka ( Georgian )
tasks.issueBody:
Hello! I’m a native Georgian (ka) speaker.

In the Georgian localization file
[ka](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/zod/src/v4/locales/ka.ts), the word "სტრინგი" is used as a translation for "string". This is not correct Georgian — სტრინგი is not a real or commonly accepted word in the language.

In Georgian validation and form-error messages, the natural and widely used term is "ველი" (field). This is the wording we consistently use in real applications and user-facing validation errors.

I suggest replacing "სტრინგი" with "ველი" throughout the Georgian locale to better match actual Georgian usage and improve translation quality.

I can create PR if the issue is approved ^_^

tasks.issueTitle: Zod triggers unsafe-eval CSP error
`z.union([])` and `z.xor([])` throw internal error on parse
tasks.issueBody:
See https://zod-playground.vercel.app?appdata=N4IgzgxgFgpgtgQxALhBA9gOzAFwASSyJ4C8eAXgHQCumAllgBQDaAugJQA6m3A9L3gzZ8heAlIVKAD3QAnFh27dZMHNVmYC0MSAA0IAG4IANtRhgUzEMEwI4MZHk4gAUuig8QAXxCt9BmFkwBkwUEAAWSgBmSgA2PRA6MAAtdAATAFk6ehQAMxMwGC8gA

The types resolve to `never`, so maybe `parse` should treat it as `never` too?

tasks.issueTitle: `transform` callback `ctx.addIssue` not in Typescript
discriminatedUnion fails on encode() when discriminator is a ZodCodec
tasks.issueBody:
## Description

When using `z.discriminatedUnion` with a discriminator field implemented as a `ZodCodec`,
`decode()` works correctly, but `encode()` fails at runtime with
`No matching discriminator`.

This happens even though:

- the types of `decode()` / `encode()` inputs are correct
- the codec itself is fully bidirectional
- the decoded domain object is valid

The issue appears to be that `discriminatedUnion` attempts to select a branch
**before encoding the discriminator field**, using the domain value instead of
the encoded (raw) value.

---

## Reproducible example

```ts
const LiteralTestCodec1 = z.codec(z.literal(1), z.literal('one'), {
    decode: (raw) => 'one' as const,
    encode: (domain) => 1 as const,
})

const LiteralTestCodec2 = z.codec(z.literal(2), z.literal('two'), {
    decode: (raw) => 'two' as const,
    encode: (domain) => 2 as const,
})

const TestUnionSchema = z.discriminatedUnion(
    'type',
    [
        z.object({
            type: LiteralTestCodec1,
            valueString: z.string(),
        }),
        z.object({
            type: LiteralTestCodec2,
            valueNumber: z.number(),
        }),
    ]
)

const domain = TestUnionSchema.decode({
    type: 1,
    valueString: 'test value',
})

const raw = TestUnionSchema.encode({
    type: 'one',
    valueString: 'test value',
})
```

---

## Actual result

`decode()` works correctly.

`encode()` throws the following error:

```json
ZodError: [
  {
    "code": "invalid_union",
    "errors": [],
    "note": "No matching discriminator",
    "discriminator": "type",
    "path": ["type"],
    "message": "Invalid input"
  }
]
```

---

## Expected behavior

Given that:

- the discriminator codec is bidirectional
- `encode()` should produce a valid raw representation

`encode()` is expected to succeed and return:

```ts
{
    type: 1,
    valueString: "test value"
}
```

---

## Notes / Workaround

At the application level, this issue can be worked around by enabling
`{ unionFallback: true }` when creating the `discriminatedUnion`.

This forces Zod to fall back to full union validation instead of the fast
discriminator-based path.

However, this behavior is surprising when combining:

- `z.discriminatedUnion`
- codecs used as discriminator fields
- `encode()` direction

---

## Possible directions

Below are concrete implementation-oriented ideas based on the current internal behavior of `ZodDiscriminatedUnion` (not prescriptions).

### 1. Make discriminator resolution context-aware

Currently, branch selection in `ZodDiscriminatedUnion` is based on a precomputed map:

```ts
const opt = disc.value.get(input?.[def.discriminator] as any);
if (opt) {
  return opt._zod.run(payload, ctx) as any;
}
```

Where `disc` is a `Map` computed once from `propValues`, derived by recursively inspecting each option schema and then cached.

For `ZodCodec`, `propValues` are defined as:

```ts
util.defineLazy(inst._zod, "propValues", () => def.in._zod.propValues);
```

This means:
- discriminator values are taken exclusively from the **`in` schema**
- there is no context available to distinguish `decode` vs `encode`
- during `encode()`, discriminator resolution still relies on *forward* (`in`) values

One possible direction would be to make `propValues` resolution context-aware, for example by turning it into a function that receives encoding direction from the parsing context (e.g. `ctx.direction: "forward" | "backward"`), allowing codecs to expose discriminator values appropriate to the current direction.

---

### 2. Introduce a backward discriminator path

An alternative (more explicit) approach would be to keep the existing fast-path intact, but add a mirrored mechanism for the backward direction:

- introduce `propValuesBackward` (mirroring `propValues`)
- derive it from `def.out` instead of `def.in` for codecs
- build and cache a separate `discBackward` map
- select between `disc` and `discBackward` based on encoding direction

This would preserve:
- current perf

tasks.issueTitle: `z.fromJSONSchema()` does not support metadata for enums, literals and not/never
z.toJSONSchema() output has non-enumerable ~standard property that breaks z.json() validation
Records do not transform keys, even though the Typescript types says it does.
tasks.issueBody:
Consider the following schema:

```typescript
z.record(
  z.literal("a").transform(() => "b" as const),
  z.string(),
);
```
(playground: https://zod-playground.vercel.app?appdata=N4IgzgxgFgpgtgQxALhALwHQCcYQPZYAmAFADoB2ABJZgDYCWALjFgrWSAqSAJQaOtyYAGYE4xYj0oBeAHyVuAI26UEYSviGMeAGgrVMYAfXIBzSXvJ8BCIaKzjCCRgkoB6AFSVklAEq4CQgAeJW4dSiMsE1N5DzcZeScXHgBuChAdEAA3NgBXGDAUAG0QYAQfbgApPChybgBfEABdTKyWMHo8chQQABYMAEZBgYyQejAALTxCAFkTehRhNjAYeqA)

I would expect it to transform the `a` key of the input into a `b`, but it doesn’t. Is it expected?

Maybe it is a design decision, but in that case there is still something wrong because when chaining a transform, the input type for the transforming function is `Record<"b", string>`, so it looks like it assumes the keys get transformed.

tasks.issueTitle: Invalid discriminator errors in Zod 4 do not list possible options like Zod 3
Falsy prefault() values are not emitted by toJsonSchema()
z.toJSONSchema() stack overflows on recursive z.lazy() union schemas when recursive branches use .describe()
z.toJSONSchema: .meta({ id }) leaks id into $defs entries — should use $id or omit it
Tuple Ignoring Default Values
tasks.issueBody:
With a defined tuple that has a fixed length of any value, default values of the property are ignored.

```js
const myTuple = z.tuple([z.string(), z.string().default('bravo')]);
const outVar = myTuple.parse(['alpha']); // results in "Invalid input: expected string, received undefined"
```

Expected response would be `['alpha', 'bravo']`. I did also try the following:

```js
const myTuple = z.tuple([z.string(), z.string()]).default([undefined, 'bravo']);
const outVar = myTuple.parse(['alpha']); // results in same error: "Invalid input: expected string, received undefined"
```

Appears to be coming from [v4/core/schemas.ts#2444](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/v4.1.8/packages/zod/src/v4/core/schemas.ts#L2444) where `if (i >= input.length) if (i >= optStart) continue;` continues out of the loop before running the parser on the optional tuple property.

This probably isn't a deal breaker as I'd expect the value to be undefined. This problem with this arises when parsing arguments for ZodFunction. There is no way to have an optional param.

Used in function context:
```js
const myFunc = z.function([z.string(), z.string().default('bravo')]).implement((name, company) => console.log(name, company)); // doesn't work

const myFunc = z.function([z.string(), z.string().default('bravo')]).implement((name, company = 'bravo') => console.log(name, company)); //  works only if defining on the async function
```

Defining a default value on the implement function does work, but seems a little anti-pattern. I'd expected a fixed length tuple to return all values or result in an error.

```js
const [ name, company ] = z.tuple([z.string(), z.string().default('bravo')]).parse(['alpha']); // doesn't fail
```

This not failing allows for company to be `undefined` when I'd be expecting a `String` value.

tasks.issueTitle: Feature Request: add `.invert()` method to codecs
`multipleOf` silently accepts non-multiples for small numbers (scientific notation bug in `floatSafeRemainder`)
tasks.issueBody:
`z.number().multipleOf(1e-7).safeParse(2.5e-7)` returns `{ success: true }`,  but `2.5e-7 / 1e-7 = 2.5`  (not a whole number).

Repro:
```
z.number().multipleOf(1e-7).safeParse(2.5e-7)  //  { success: true } — should be false
z.number().multipleOf(1e-7).safeParse(3.3e-7)  // { success: true } — should be false
z.number().multipleOf(1e-7).safeParse(3e-7)    // { success: true } — correct
```

Root cause: `floatSafeRemainder` in util.ts counts decimal places by splitting `val.toString()` on ".". For small numbers, JS uses scientific notation: (2.5e-7).toString() gives "2.5e-7", so the fractional part is "5e-7" (length 4) instead of the actual 8 decimal places. 

The function already handles this for the step parameter (lines 251-255) but not for val.

Suggested fix: Apply the same scientific notation regex to val.toString().

Zod version: 4.3.6

tasks.issueTitle: [Zod v4.3+] Cannot use 'in' operator to search for 'description' in undefined
tasks.issueBody:
I apologize if you were already aware of this.

When I tried running it using the latest zod and zod-openapi, the following error occurred.

```
{
    "type": "TypeError",
    "message": "Cannot use 'in' operator to search for 'description' in undefined",
    "stack":
        TypeError: Cannot use 'in' operator to search for 'description' in undefined
            at flattenRef (/path/node_modules/.pnpm/zod@4.3.5/node_modules/zod/v4/core/to-json-schema.js:260:29)
            at finalize (/path/node_modules/.pnpm/zod@4.3.5/node_modules/zod/v4/core/to-json-schema.js:296:9)
            at toJSONSchema (/path/node_modules/.pnpm/zod@4.3.5/node_modules/zod/v4/core/json-schema-processors.js:590:28)
            at createSchemas (/path/node_modules/.pnpm/zod-openapi@5.4.6_zod@4.3.5/node_modules/zod-openapi/dist/components-DiNDbisK.mjs:378:21)
            at createIOSchemas (/path/node_modules/.pnpm/zod-openapi@5.4.6_zod@4.3.5/node_modules/zod-openapi/dist/components-DiNDbisK.mjs:883:42)
            at createComponents (/path/node_modules/.pnpm/zod-openapi@5.4.6_zod@4.3.5/node_modules/zod-openapi/dist/components-DiNDbisK.mjs:903:2)
            at Object.fastifyZodOpenApiTransformObject [as transformObject] (/path/node_modules/.pnpm/fastify-zod-openapi@5.5.0_@fastify+swagger@9.6.1_fastify@5.6.2_zod@4.3.5/node_modules/fastify-zod-openapi/dist/index.mjs:437:22)
            at Object.swagger (/path/node_modules/.pnpm/@fastify+swagger@9.6.1/node_modules/@fastify/swagger/lib/spec/openapi/index.js:74:17)
            at Object.<anonymous> (/path/packages/backend/core/src/fastify/plugin/common/zodOpenApiPlugin.ts:87:28)
}
```

The error in `src/transformer.test.ts` may be related.

```

    +       },
    +     },
    +   },
    +   "info": {
    +     "title": "hello world",
    +     "version": "1.0.0",
    +   },
    +   "openapi": "3.0.3",
    +   "paths": {
    +     "/": {
    +       "post": {

```

P.S. Thank you for the quick fix to zod-openapi today.
